commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Xiaomi Token Plan provider id to the bundled Xiaomi plugin, including regional auth choices, model catalog/pricing metadata, config/usage wiring, tests, and Xiaomi docs updates.

PR surface: Source +563, Tests +285, Docs +75. Total +923 across 21 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows catalog gating only checks provider object existence, while the PR accepts blank-baseUrl overlays and has an SGP manifest default. I did not run a local runtime repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: A narrow repair can fix the endpoint-gating and docs-pricing blockers, while maintainer approval remains required for the new provider/auth surface.

target.title: feat:Add Xiaomi Token Plan provider support
target.body: ## Summary - add first-class `xiaomi-token-plan` support to the bundled Xiaomi provider plugin - add regional Token Plan onboarding/auth choices (China/Europe/Singapore), static v2.5 catalog entries, and `tp-...` / `sk-...` key-shape validation - wire the new provider through usage/auth/config overlay surfaces and update Xiaomi provider docs - set model costs with tiered cache-read pricing: flat input/output per 1M tokens, cache-read pricing steps at 256K input tokens - use model catalog names as display aliases instead of generic provider labels - keep release-owned `CHANGELOG.md` unchanged (per maintainer policy) ## Why OpenClaw's bundled Xiaomi plugin only supported pay-as-you-go `xiaomi` setup, while issue #86169 asked for Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan support without requiring manual custom-provider wiring. ## Verification - 7 test files, 165 tests passed at `bb7148c52f` (`extensions/xiaomi/`, `src/commands/auth-choice-options`, `src/config/config-misc`, `src/infra/provider-usage.*`) ## Screenshots ## Model costs | Model | Input | Output | cacheRead (<256K) | cacheRead (>=256K) | |---|---|---|---|---| | `mimo-v2.5-pro` | $1/1M | $3/1M | $0.20/1M | $0.40/1M | | `mimo-v2.5` | $0.4/1M | $2/1M | $0.08/1M | $0.16/1M | Prices are USD per million tokens. Cache-read pricing uses `tieredPricing` with a step at 256,000 input tokens. Config example in `docs/providers/xiaomi.md` matches the runtime pricing catalog. ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: OpenClaw can onboard and resolve Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan as a first-class provider with regional preset endpoints. Real environment tested: macOS local source checkout at `bb7148c52f` with live Xiaomi Token Plan China (`cn`) region `tp-...` API key against `https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1`. Exact steps and output at bb7148c52f: 1. `curl -s "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1/models"` — HTTP 200, 8 models: `['mimo-v2-omni', 'mimo-v2-pro', 'mimo-v2-tts', 'mimo-v2.5', 'mimo-v2.5-pro', 'mimo-v2.5-tts', 'mimo-v2.5-tts-voiceclone', 'mimo-v2.5-tts-voicedesign']` 2. Chat completion `mimo-v2.5-pro`: `model: mimo-v2.5-pro`, `usage: prompt=255, completion=20, cacheRead=192`, `finish: length` 3. Chat completion `mimo-v2.5`: `model: mimo-v2.5`, `usage: prompt=253, completion=10`, `finish: length` 4. `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model "xiaomi-token-plan/mimo-v2.5" --message "Reply with ok and nothing else" --thinking off --json`: `provider: xiaomi-token-plan, model: mimo-v2.5, fallback: False`, `text: ok`, `usage: input=90, output=2, cacheRead=22656` Observed result after fix: Provider resolves correctly, models respond, cache hits are counted, costs flow from manifest to pricing catalog, and the model dropdown shows specific model names ("Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro") instead of generic labels. What was not tested: The `ams` (Europe) and `sgp` (Singapore) regional endpoints, GitHub Actions / Crabbox / Testbox CI runs. Fixes #86169.
target.author: nianjiuzst

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
